entry.title: My experience during a SAP technical upgrade (Pre-implementation & Post-Implementation)
entry.content:
A typical SAP upgrade project would require technical as well as functional adjustments/modifications, which any implementation team would need to follow. The following blog discusses mainly on few technical aspects, a technical consultant (Mainly an ABAP consultant) would need to follow, which are based on experience on multiple system upgrades that I was involved in.

This blog covers from the pre-implementation phase, to the post-implementation phase, where it also contains information with regards to the SPDD/SPAU corrections and modifications.

Pre-implementation


Basis consultants would follow a set of processes during the system preparation process, which includes data backups and many other tasks. Once these steps are completed, the basis consultant would begin the upgrade via the software upgrade manager (SUM).

The typical steps in the SUM (Figure 1) would be as follows.

  1. Extraction

  2. Configuration

  3. Checks

  4. Preprocessing

  5. Execution

  6. Postprocessing



Figure 1: Software Update Manager



Open transactions in SPDD/SPAU



  • At first, the involvement of an ABAP consultant would be required during the Preprocessing phase. Here, the system would first check for any open transactions in SPDD and SPAU, which we need to correct, in order to proceed to the next step (Figure 2). This is not a mandatory correction that we need to do as the system would prompt again with the same list of open transactions during the actual SPDD/SPAU phase. But I would recommend to correct them here as it would reduce the number of corrections that we need to do in the next steps.



Figure 2 - Open Transactions in SPDD/SPAU



Inactive objects



  • Once the above corrections are made, again it would prompt a warning message if there are any inactive objects in the system (Figure 3).



Figure 3 - Preprocessing Inactive Objects




  • The ABAP consultant must either activate all these objects, or delete them from the system, in order to proceed to the next step in the software upgrade manager.


Note: This error would typically come if there are any inactive objects under local objects of system users. If activating them is not possible due to any reason, I would recommend deleting them as they are anyway local objects and it can do no harm to the systems in either Quality or Production environments.

If the inactive objects are in transport requests, they must be activated, and the respective requests must be released.

Errors causing to repeat the above phase



  • Once the above steps are completed, if there are any corrections yet to be made in the system, these will be populated in the ACTUPG.ELG file, which will be available in the SAP directory (AL11). This would contain a typical set of error as follows, which needs to be corrected by the ABAP consultant.



  1. Removing duplicate table indexes.

  2. Activation and adjustments of search helps that contains errors.

  3. Removal of duplicated fields in table enhancements.



  • Given below is an example of how the errors would be displayed in the SUM (Figure 4), where the BASIS consultant could also provide the set of errors which needs to be corrected in an extracted PDF document.



Figure 4 - Repeat Phase




  • Once the above corrections are made, the SUM should run without any errors, which would then come to the next step in doing repository modifications in the Shadow system.


Note: Make sure to maintain a separate transport request for the corrections under the above steps, as the software component version for the corrections done until now would be lower, compared to the version that would be available after the system upgrade. If you miss this, there could be complications while importing the transport request to the systems that would be upgraded in quality and production environments.

Repository Modifications (SPDD)



  • The next step during the upgrade would be to do the repository modifications under transaction SPDD, where the SUM would populate a message as follows (Figure 5).



Figure 5 - Repository Modifications


 

  • A detailed guideline on how to carry out the SPDD phase could be found in the following blog, which has been posted by arjun.pawar .The information given in the blog is really helpful for a consultant to understand the entire SPDD phase.


https://blogs.sap.com/2013/10/14/technical-upgrade-spdd-spau-phase/

Note: Make sure to maintain a separate transaction request for the SPDD corrections, as the software component version of the system is higher than the previous level now. You cannot import this transaction to the systems quality and production environments, as the system have not been upgraded yet and the software component over there is old.

 

Post-implementation


Once the pre-implementation steps are completed, the BASIC consultant would continue to upgrade the system via the SUM, where the next step would be to do the adjustment of modified repository objects (Figure 6).

Modified Repository Objects (SPAU)



Figure 6 - Adjustment of Modified Repository Objects




  • A detailed guideline on how to carry out corrections and modifications under the SPAU transaction could be found in the following blog, which is the same blog as mentioned in the SPDD phase.


https://blogs.sap.com/2013/10/14/technical-upgrade-spdd-spau-phase/

 

Important thins to know while committing SPAU corrections/adjustments.



  • Always maintain a separate transaction request for the SPAU phase.

  • The system will be open for 14 working days, for any modifications.

  • Make sure to confirm all obsolete notes, after having a discussion with relevant owners (Consultants who implemented the notes). Sometimes they would want to re-import the objects under the note, even though it is obsolete.

  • Make sure to check whether the objects under the delete tab should be deleted or re-implemented. Sometimes you may require them again. As an example, if any third part packages have been imported to the system, they will be marked as deleted objects during the upgrade. I'm pretty sure you will want to have those objects even after the upgrade.

  • Make sure to get everything tested (A full cycle test) within the given 14 days. The technical consultant does not know what processes would be impacted after the corrections are been made and it is the process owners’ responsibility to make sure that all processes used by the business runs without interruptions.


Note: A functional overview of the upgrade process could be found in the following blog, which has been posted by n.palanisami , which I found very interesting and important in grabbing the above said knowledge and experience from a functional perspective.

https://blogs.sap.com/2012/11/16/sap-technical-upgrade-and-sap-ehp-a-functional-overview/

 

I hope the above experience gained through the system upgrades, would be helpful for anyone who is involved in a SAP upgrade. I know for a fact, it is not easy to do a proper SAP upgrade if you are not familiar with the above steps. And getting familiar with it is not an easy task as SAP technical upgrades are not carried out frequently, for the same system.

Keep me posted with any questions you have with regards to the above mentioned content, I will be adding more such experiences in near future.

Thank You!

entry.title: Live Now! SAP BTP DevOps Podcast Series
entry.content:
So what's the news?


Exciting times! 📢📢📢

I am glad to announce the launch of our SAP BTP DevOps Podcast Series! 🚀🚀🚀

This month's episode features our lead product manager for SAP BTP DevOps, boris.zarske , who will take us through the overview of our offerings and talk about what makes SAP BTP DevOps special, especially when you are just starting out in the SAP BTP environment.

You can find this month's episode here 👉🏻👉🏻👉🏻 : Episode 1

Cool! What comes next?


The overall series will focus on SAP's offerings in the DevOps space for SAP Business Technology Platform and beyond!

In the following episodes, we will talk about more specific areas like CI/CD, Delivery and Change Management in Hybrid Landscapes, and Operations Automation.

Each month I will focus on a different topic and invite an expert from that area to introduce us to the topic and answer the top questions for that particular topic.

This is also a chance for you (our listeners and SAP DevOps practitioners) to get your questions answered. So please feel free to reach out to me if you have any questions! 🙏🏻


What else?


You can reach out to Boris on Linkedin  here

You can reach me on LinkedIn here

You can check out our DevOps community here

For any questions/feedback, please feel free to reach out to me or leave a comment below.

I'll keep you posted as each episode is released. Looking forward to your impressions.

Stay tuned and have fun listening 🎧 🙂

entry.title: Your Chance to Influence SAP BTP Products and Tools
entry.content:
📢📢📢Call for Registrations 📢📢📢

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative #2021🤝🏻👔

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative gives customers the opportunity to influence SAP software development decisions and adopt new innovations early on. 👨‍💻🤝

Under this initiative, I have created a project where SAP customers and partners shall have the opportunity to help shape and influence the lifecycle of various types of application and configuration content on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

Please make use of this great opportunity to influence our lifecycle management, software logistics, and DevOps processes (such as around deployment, change/transport management and observability) on SAP Business Technology Platform!

And don't forget to spread the word! 😀 🙌


Register here 👉👉👉 : Registration Link

entry.title: Episode 2 Live Now! | SAP BTP DevOps Podcast Series
entry.content:
Great! What are we talking about in Episode 2?


An exciting interview with my friend and colleague irina.kirilova! 📢📢📢

Irina is a Product Manager in the SAP Business Technology Platform area focusing on Continuous Integration and Delivery topics, and has been with SAP for more than two decades now! 😎😎😎

Irina and I talk about the CI/CD story in the context of the SAP Business Technology Platform, with topics like ‘Getting started with CI/CD’,  ‘SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery, and ‘Project Piper’.

We also talk about making important decisions like when to use which CI/CD tool, why customers should care about CI/CD, and some best practices. 🙌🏻

You can find this month’s episode here 👉🏻👉🏻👉🏻 : Episode 2

Cool! What comes next?


Like I mentioned last time, the overall series will focus on SAP’s offerings in the DevOps space for SAP Business Technology Platform and beyond!

In next month's episode, we will talk about a topic that's of strategic importance to most of SAP's customers: DevOps in the ABAP world!

This is also a chance for you (our listeners and SAP DevOps practitioners) to get your questions answered. So please feel free to reach out to me if you have any questions! 🙏🏻

What else?


You can reach out to Irina on Linkedin  here

You can reach me on LinkedIn here

You can check out our DevOps community here

For any questions/feedback, please feel free to reach out to me or leave a comment below.

I’ll keep you posted as each episode is released. Looking forward to your impressions.

Stay tuned and have fun listening 🎧 🙂

entry.title: Episode 3 is out! | ABAP DevOps | SAP BTP DevOps Podcast Series
entry.content:
Awesome! What's in the spotlight in Episode 3?


An exciting interview with my friend and colleague karin.spiegel! 📢📢📢

Karin is a Product Manager in the SAP Cloud and Lifecycle Management area focusing on topics related to change and transport management in the ABAP and non-ABAP worlds.  😎😎😎

Karin and I talk about the BTP ABAP Environment, the importance of DevOps in the ABAP world, CI/CD options for the SAP BTP ABAP environment, and many more topics. 🙌🏻

You can find this month’s episode here 👉🏻👉🏻👉🏻 : Episode 3

Great! What comes next?


Like I mentioned last time, the overall series will focus on SAP’s offerings in the DevOps space for SAP Business Technology Platform and beyond!

In next month’s episode, we will talk about a topic that’s gaining more and more importance, as we are moving from an on-premise to a hybrid or cloud-focused world of enterprise software: Delivery and Change Management!

This is also a chance for you (our listeners and SAP DevOps practitioners) to get your questions answered. So please feel free to reach out to me if you have any questions! 🙏🏻

I’ll keep you posted as each episode is released. Looking forward to your impressions.

Stay tuned and have fun listening 🎧 🙂

entry.title: Get Certified: C_TADM_21 | SAP Certified Technology Associate - SAP S/4HANA System Administration
entry.content:
In this blog, you will find some personal suggestions about how to get certified as SAP Certified Technology Associate - SAP S/4HANA System Administration.

This exam is available as of June 2021.

For the Fiori system administration exam, see

  • SAP Certified Technology Associate – SAP Fiori System Administration


For the previous version of the certification (and exam), see

  • SAP Certified Technology Associate – System Administration (SAP HANA) with SAP NetWeaver 7.5


For the posts about the SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 05 exams, see

  • SAP Certified Technology Associate – SAP HANA 2.0 SPS05 (TEC | IMP | DEV)


For the posts about the ABAP specialisation exam, see

  • SAP Certified Development Specialist – ABAP for SAP HANA 2.0


About braindumps and cheat sheets, see

  • 100% Free Blogpost about SAP HANA (Braindumps and Cheat Sheets)


Questions? Post as comment.

Useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Thanks!


Once you passed the exam, you will receive a digital badge from Credly (Acclaim).

  • SAP Certified Technology Associate - SAP S/4HANA System Administration


See the FAQ for more information about SAP Global Certification Digital badges.


About the Exam 


Associate Level


Information about the exam is published on the SAP Training website. This is also where you can book the exam (or get a Certification Hub subscription).

  • training.sap.com/certification/c_tadm_21


TADM is the code for Technical Administration. The C_ stands for the associate (consultant) level type exam.

Validity


Previous editions of this certification still valid and available are

  • C_TADM55a_75 = SAP HANA 2.0

  • C_TADM54_75 = SAP ASE


The editions for other databases and previous SAP NetWeaver versions have expired.

You can check the validity of the certifications at training.sap.com/certification/validity.

For the certification FAQs, see

  • SAP Global Certification FAQ – Overview

  • SAP Global Certification FAQ – Exam Process

  • SAP Global Certification FAQ – Post-Exam Process


SAP Certification Hub


To take the exam visit SAP Training and select Access the Certification Hub.

  • training.sap.com/access




Topic Areas


Exam Page


Information about the topic areas covered is listed on the exam information page together with the corresponding SAP training courses, either the Academy track or the individual courses (see below).


 






Note the small print: "SAP reserves the right to update the exam content (topics, items, weighting) at any time" make sure to check the exam page before applying.

What's New


Compared with the previous edition, the topics areas are similar except that the "minor" topics System Administration (AS Java) and User Administration (AS ABAP and/or AS Java) now have been merged into a "major" topic area: System Administration (AS ABAP; AS Java Basics; User Management Basics).

The topic area Browser-based UI is now labeled Technology Components for HTTP-based Communication but still covers the same material: Configure ICM and install and configure SAP Web Dispatcher.

The software release has been updated to SAP S/4HANA 1909 (from SAP NetWeaver 7.5).

For the details about how SAP NetWeaver evolved into the ABAP Platform (not covered in the exam), see

  • ABAP Platform – Part 1 – Evolution from SAP Netweaver





How to Prepare


SAP Training and SAP Learning Hub


The C_TADM certification is entirely based on SAP training courses and although not formally listed as a requirement, it will be challenging to pass the exam without taking any classes (be it in classroom or e-learning format).

The topic areas are covered in the following courses (consultant academy trainings).

  • TADM10 (SAP S/4HANA 1909) | 10 days

  • TADM12 (SAP S/4HANA 1909) | 10 days

  • TADM55 (SAP HANA 2.0 SPS05) | 5 days


Alternatively, you can follow the track from the learning journey but this takes will take you a couple days more as ADM800 covers AS Java in more detail than the technical academy courses.

If classroom training brings you bad memories, consider a SAP Learning Hub subscription. There is a trial edition available for you to try it out.

  • SAP Learning Hub



Included with the training is access to a test environment with exercises.


The learning journey shows a visualisation of the courses to take. You can logon and mark your progress.

  • SAP S/4HANA - Technical Implementation & Operations (Administrator | Consultant)


Note that there is a lot of overlap between SAPTEC, recommended as prerequisites for some courses, and ADM100.


The tiles for each of the courses show the available formats: classroom and e-books. When subscribed to the learning hub, you also have access to an SAP HANA system for hands-on exercises (Live Access). The assessment contains the sample questions from the course.



TADM55 corresponds to the HA200 training. If you already participated in this training or passed the C_HANATEC exam, no need to join this course again


Additional Resources


SAP Press


There is no SAP Certification Success Guide for the C_TADM exam (yet) for the exam as a whole. However, the Database Administration topic area (course TADM55 / HA200) is covered in

  • SAP HANA 2.0 Certification Guide: Technology Associate Exam




For the exam, see the blog post

  • SAP Certified Technology Associate – SAP HANA 2.0 SPS05


For more detailed coverage, beyond the scope of the exam, see

  • SAP S/4HANA Architecture | 2020

  • SAP Gateway and OData | 2019


There are also some older books about SAP NetWeaver administration still in print and available covering earlier software releasesf.

  • SAP Administration | 2015

  • SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP—System Administration | 2012


Related but for a different persona and exam, are the certification success guides

  • ABAP 7.5 Certification Guide covering C_TAW12_750 | 2018

  • SAP Fiori Certification Guide covering C_FIORDEV_21 | 2021


SAP Help Portal


Although most material covered in the training courses is also documented "somewhere" in many of the product guides on the SAP Help Portal, trying to pass the exam on documentation study alone is not very realistic. However, doing some extra study by searching for some key terminology and relevant parts in the guides might be helpful.

  • SAP S/4HANA 1909

  • SAP HANA Platform 2.0 SPS 05



SAP Notes and Knowledge Base


In the training manuals and documentation many references are made to SAP Notes and knowledge base articles (KBAs). Although the articles are not part of the study material, reading the notes is always a good idea to strengthen your understanding. You need a S-user account to access the launchpad.

  • SAP Support One Launchpad Knowledge Base Search | e.g. filter on BC (Basis Components) and Knowledge Base Articles.




Sample Questions


On the exam page, there is a link to a trial exam with 15 sample questions which closely resembles the real exam experience.

If it is obvious what the answer should be for all questions, you are ready to take the exam. If you have no clue, you might want to study some more.



Share and Connect


Questions? Please post as comment.

Useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Thanks!

If you would like to receive updates, connect with me on

  • LinkedIn > linkedin.com/in/dvankempen

  • Twitter > @dvankempen


For the author page of SAP PRESS, visit

  • Denys van Kempen | SAP PRESS









Over the years, for the SAP HANA Academy, SAP’s Partner Innovation Lab, and à titre personnel, I have written a little over 300 posts here for the SAP Community. Some articles only reached a few readers. Others attracted quite a few more.

For your reading pleasure and convenience, here is a curated list of posts which somehow managed to pass the 10k-view mile stone and, as sign of current interest, still tickle the counters each month.

  • Good Reads (my two cents)

entry.title: Updates to enhanced Maintenance Planner
entry.content:
As you might be aware, Maintenance Planner got enhanced with some new features in July 2020. These include:  

  • Hybrid Landscape Visualization

  • Product Analytics 

  • New Cloud Integration Automation planning 

  • New SAP Fiori theme



                                                    Home page of Maintenance Planner 

These enhancements are running on SAP Business Technology Platform infrastructure (see also SAP Business Technology Platform Regions, technical key ‘cf-eu10’), which runs on AWS. During initial rollout we followed ticket-based approach where customers were required to create an onboarding ticket and provide explicit consent for data replication and further handling and storage. 

Starting September 2021, we now support seamless onboarding/experience for most existing customers using the maintenance planner. Onboarding will be done in phases in the month of September. For more details, refer the updated SAP Note 2921927 and FAQ SAP Note 2973010.  

For customers who have not been onboarded automatically and intend to use the new capabilities, the ticket-based onboarding is still applicable.  

 Further information 

  • Short video giving you a glimpse of new innovations 

  • Refer SAP Note 2921927 to learn more about rollout and onboarding 

  • SAP Help Portal 

  • SAP Support Portal Page 

  • SAP Community Blog: Visualize System Landscapes using Maintenance Planner  



  • SAP TechEd 2020 replay: Navigate Your Cloud Journey with Maintenance Planner  

  • SAP Community webinar replay

entry.title: Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA is Now Generally Available
entry.content:
In this blog I describe the general availability of Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA.

Abstract: With SAP S/4HANA 2021 and SUM 2.0 SP 12, the Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA is generally available. This blog describes the prerequisites to successfully leverage ZDO for SAP S/4HANA.

Initial version (November 17, 2021): Blog published

Minor update (January 20, 2022): Youtube link to SAP TechEd 2021 replay added

Training material update (June 17, 2022): ADM330e and ADM330k have been re-released with updated content

Training material update (April 29, 2023): ADM330e and ADM330k have been replaced by new SAP Learning Journey Leverage SAP S/4HANA Updates and Upgrades with Zero Downtime Option of SUM on SAP Learning


Overview


Until now, Zero Downtime Option of Software Update Manager for SAP S/4HANA was only available to pilot customers like described in the SAP Community blog Leveraging Zero Downtime Option of SUM for SAP S/4HANA release upgrades and updates.








We're pleased to announce that as of November 17, 2021, Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA is generally available.


 

Availability and Boundary Conditions


Let's take a look to the availability in terms of supported products and add-ons and how you can get educated on Zero Downtime Option.


General availability of the Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA


First: the general availability of ZDO for SAP S/4HANA applies to systems running SAP S/4HANA 2020 or higher. There, ZDO can be used to perform major release upgrades as well as feature or support package updates.

Besides SAP S/4HANA 2020 (or higher), also SAP S/4HANA Foundation 2020 (or higher) is supported by ZDO. Please note that the product SAP S/4HANA Foundation is not allowed to be used standalone but only as a foundation in combination with a defined list of add-on product versions. Further details can be found in the release information SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 2912919 - SAP S/4HANA FOUNDATION 2020: Release Information Note

  • SAP Note 3015497 - SAP S/4HANA FOUNDATION 2021: Release Information Note


The supported update and upgraded paths to utilize Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA are described and visualized in SAP Note 2707731 which is the comprehensive entry point to get all relevant information before starting a ZDO project.

Second: SAP S/4HANA supports add-ons from different vendors like SAP or 3rd party:

  • Standard add-ons delivered by SAP for SAP S/4HANA

  • Add-ons delivered by SAP Innovative Business Solution Organization (former SAP Custom
    Development)

  • Partner add-ons


For details, see SAP Note 2214409 - SAP S/4HANA: Compatible Add-Ons.

However, not all add-ons available for SAP S/4HANA implicitly are equipped with zero-downtime maintenance capability. Hence, Software Update Manager is equipped with an automated check that detects if all products and add-ons installed in the system are supported by ZDO. The full list of add-ons supported by ZDO is attached to the central SAP Note 2707731. If ZDO is not possible because of addon products in the system, potentially a custom-specific project can be started.

Third: Along with the general availability, the SAP Learning Journey Leverage SAP S/4HANA Updates and Upgrades with Zero Downtime Option of SUM on SAP Learning was launched. Both, the e-learning, as well as the assessment are mandatory in order to get started using ZDO. The two new training modules are part of the Learning Journey "Software Lifecycle Management Tools".

After graduating from the mandatory SAP Learning Journey Leverage SAP S/4HANA Updates and Upgrades with Zero Downtime Option of SUM on SAP Learning, you'll receive a digital badge from credly.com.

By having the digital badge, you'll be eligible to request a password which is asked by Software Update Manager after choosing the Scenario Strategy Zero Downtime Option (ZDO):


Selection of ZDO in Software Update Manager 2.0 SP 12 and higher


 

Summary



  • Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) does not require an additional license as it comes along with the standard tool for maintenance: Software Update Manager.

  • ZDO for SAP S/4HANA reduces the overall business downtime for maintenance events like upgrades and updates.

  • From SAP S/4HANA 2020, ZDO is generally available. For details on prerequisites and restrictions, see SAP Note 2707731.

  • The SAP Learning Journey Leverage SAP S/4HANA Updates and Upgrades with Zero Downtime Option of SUM on SAP Learning.


 

More Information


For more information, you can refer to the following sources:

  • SAP Note 2707731 – Prerequisites and restrictions of Zero Downtime Option of SUM for SAP S/4HANA

  • SAP Support Portal page on Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA

  • SAP Community Blog posts

    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA 2021 Product Release

    • Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) of SUM – Project Planning



  • Replay of SAP TechEd 2021: DT111 – Enjoy a Next-Generation, Zero-Downtime Experience with SAP S/4HANA Upgrades

  • Replay of SAP TechEd 2020: DT111 – Discover Zero Downtime Maintenance for Upgrading SAP S/4HANA

  • Replay of SAP TechEd 2019: CAA301 – Leveraging Zero-Downtime Maintenance for SAP S/4HANA

  • Replay of SAP TechEd 2018: CNA306 – Get Your SAP S/4HANA Ready for Running a Zero-Downtime Update Project


 

Jens Fieger

Product Management SAP SE, Software Logistics

entry.title: Note Assistant Made Easy
entry.content:
Happy New Year to everyone! I am delighted to share some great news to start off this Year. The well-known Note Assistant tool is now more intuitive, simplified, customizable and provides a new user experience!!  Watch the video giving you a glimpse of the enhancements.

The Note Assistant (transaction SNOTE) enables you to download, display, implement and de-implement SAP Notes. Note Assistant has been there for quite a while and has gone through as many enhancements. Over the last few years, we have been consolidating feedback from our customers and end users, in addition to which we collaborated closely with customers through a Customer engagement initiative (CEI) project. As part of CEI, we conducted customer interviews and workshops to gather concrete feedback, outside of which our experiences based on  customer support and internal feedback was also considered. Based on the comprehensive feedback, we worked on the design and revalidated the same through CEI engagements. At DSAG 2019 Usability testing of the enhancements gave us fruitful insights and we received extensive feedback. Our aim has been to address these collective pain points in the revamped tool.

Highlights of key enhancements

  • A new Home screen,

    • Single screen integrating Worklist and Note Browser

    • The worklist and search results are displayed in tabs to improve usability





Note Assistant Home Screen




  • Streamlined SAP Notes Implementation



SAP Notes Implementation




  • Enhanced SAP Notes display screen



Enhanced SAP Notes display screen




  • Note Assistant User Settings



User settings


How to Update to revamped Note Assistant

  • Enhancements are available from SAP_BASIS 7.50 SP0 onwards, automatically or by implementing a SAP Note

  • Implement SAP Note 3051466  (valid from SAP_BASIS 7.50 SP0) to update your system to revamped Note Assistant. For details please refer implementation guide attached to SAP Note 3051466.

  • Enhancements are included in the below SAP_BASIS Release/Support Packages:

    • SAP_BASIS 7.56 SP00

    • SAP_BASIS 7.55 SP03

    • SAP_BASIS 7.54 SP05

    • SAP_BASIS 7.53 SP07

    • SAP_BASIS 7.52 SP09

    • SAP_BASIS 7.51 SP13

    • SAP_BASIS 7.50 SP22





  • For more information, Please refer: 

    • PDF documents attached to SAP Note 3051466

    • FAQ  SAP Note 3131774

    • Webinar replay




With this announcement we wish you a great start to 2022 and hope the enhancements to the Note Assistant make your experience a better one!!

entry.title: Introducing the ZDO Preliminary Checks as part of SUM Toolbox
entry.content:
Introduction


Software Update Manager (SUM) Toolbox is an ABAP-based transaction that allows you to run certain tools and reports related to the various scenarios offered by Software Update Manager.
Starting from release 05/2022 of SUM Toolbox, the ZDO Preliminary Checks have been delivered. The main idea of the checks is to verify if a system can be upgraded or updated using Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) even before starting Software Update Manager.

Which Checks Are Performed?


As part of the SUM Toolbox, the ZDO preliminary checks are delivered with the focus on the consistency of the following topics:

  • Usage of SAP Business Warehouse

  • Status of Silent Data Migration Infrastructure

  • Consistency check of data dictionary and database

  • Consistency check of SAP HANA content deployment

  • Consistency check of active nametab objects

  • Check ZDO compliance of SLT setup


 


SUM Toolbox: ZDO Preliminary Checks Entry Screen


 

Usage of SAP Business Warehouse


The usage of SAP Business Warehouse (BW) can differ dependent on the applications used in the SAP S/4HANA system. SAP BW distinguishes between three different operating modes:

  • Data Warehouse

  • Embedded BW

  • Embedded Analytics


This check requires the following SAP Notes to be applied beforehand:

  • SAP Note 3006375 - BW operating mode and zero downtime maintenance

  • SAP Note 3098968 - BW operating mode and zero downtime maintenance

  • SAP Note 3154703 - Enhance CL_RS_UTILITIES=>GET_SYSTEM_SCOPE


If a critical object like an Info Cube, Data Store, or Advanced Data Store is found, these object would have to be checked in detail as they potentially leverage data warehousing which is not supported by ZDO.

Note:
If a ZDO update or upgrade is blocked because of determining data warehousing in the SAP BW check,

create an incident via SAP ONE Support Launchpad for component BC-UPG-DTM-TLA.

Exemplary result:


ZDO Preliminary Checks: BW Check


 

Status of Silent Data Migration Infrastructure


Typically, Silent Data Migration Infrastructure (SDMI) classes have to be finished in all clients prior an upgrade is started. However, there might be also SDMI classes with an extended release validity. This check outlines the SDMI classes which must be finished before starting the ZDO upgrade procedure. For more details, see SAP Note 2907976 - Silent Data Migration (SDMI) - FAQ.

Exemplary result:


ZDO Preliminary Checks: SDMI Check


 

Consistency Check of Data Dictionary And Database


During a ZDO update or upgrade, all database and data dictionary objects have to be in a consistent state. The check as part of SUM Toolbox determines a broad spectrum of potential erroneous objects like:

  • Missing tables and indexes in data dictionary but present in database

  • Inconsistent indexes, views, and tables


Exemplary result:


ZDO Preliminary Checks: DDIC Check


 

Consistency Check of SAP HANA Content Deployment


This check validates the consistency of all objects in SAP HANA content deployed by HANA Transport Container (HTC), SAP HANA Transport for ABAP (HTA), and SAP HANA Deployment Infrastructure (HDI). During the ZDO procedure, the HDI containers need to be re-deployed for the bridge sub-system. Hence, all objects have to be in a consistent state. Also, this check lists the custom-created objects created in SAP HANA Repository which require a manual migration into an HDI container.

Exemplary result:


ZDO Preliminary Checks: HANA Content Check


 

Consistency Check of Active Nametab Objects


The nametab (short for name table) or sometimes also called runtime object is read by the kernel in all places when Information about ABAP Dictionary objects is requested. Inconsistent nametabs can have an impact on the ZDO procedure, especially in case tables are classified as cloned tables. Hence, this check provides a consistency check if all active nametab objects have a corresponding object in the ABAP Dictionary. Also, it's checked if the ABAP dictionary object belongs to a valid and consistent database table.

Exemplary result:


ZDO Preliminary Checks: Nametab Check


 

Check ZDO Compliance of SLT setup


ZDO supports RFC-based SLT replication. It's essential that the SLT setup is fully consistent. In case SLT triggers are attached to a database table, a logging table must exist in the ABAP Dictionary as well as on database level. The same hold true for the other way round: if a logging table exists, SLT triggers must be present in the database.

Exemplary result:


ZDO Preliminary Checks: SLT Check


 

When And Where Should The Checks be Executed?


It's recommended to run the ZDO Preliminary Checks in all systems which should be upgraded or updated using ZDO. Of course, it's important to start with the sandbox system as outlined in the ZDO e-Learning ADM330e (linked here: SAP Support Portal page on Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA). The sandbox should be created out of a recent copy of your production system to get the most value out of the checks.

More Information



  • SAP Community blog posts:

    • Software Update Manager Toolbox is Available Now

    • Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA is Now Generally Available



  • SAP Notes:

    • SAP Note 3092738 - Software Update Manager Toolbox – Central SAP Note

    • SAP Note 2707731 - Prerequisites and restrictions of Zero Downtime Option of SUM for SAP S/4HANA



  • SAP Support Portal page on Zero Downtime Option for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: HA/DR Architecture on SAP Shared File system on Windows in Azure Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction: This blog explains a reference architecture of a High available SAP system in Windows in Azure Cloud  with DR solution. This is one of the recommended architectures from Microsoft & SAP in Windows in Azure cloud where we will be utilizing Windows DFS-N to support flexible SAP shared file systems.

We will be referring shared file system as below, but same architecture can also be followed in case we have any other shared file system (e.g Interfaces which we would like to have in both regions):

  1. \\<ANF or AFS name >\sapmnt

  2. \\<ANF or AFS name >\trans


Scenario: We can refer this in below scenario:

  • Implementation of SAP systems in High available scenario

  • Configuring SAP Disaster Recovery Solution

  • Operating System will be Windows

  • Azure Site Recovery (ASR) is used for creating Application VMs in DR Region


Benefits:

  • Significant reduction of RTO during Disaster

  • Controlling number of characters on hostname which has limitations (Refer SAP NOTE: 611361,2718300)


Motivation:  

Here is a high level architecture (See Fig 1):

  • ASCS, ERS, PAS and AS are part of Application Subnet which will access their sapmnt or trans directory configured in AFS or ANF which is accessible in that region.

  • AFS or ANF has been provisioned with their physical name as result of that all the profiles, registry, Configtools parameters, interfaces will point to that physical name.

  • Database subnets are not shown below as they used local FS ( e.g /hana/data, /hana/log) and async mode replication is configured between primary and secondary region.



                                                         Fig 1

Now during Disaster, as per recommendation from Microsoft Azure, ASR will be used to provision VMs in secondary DR region as below (Check Fig 2):


                                                          Fig 2

Now, here it comes the difficulties as with ASR , all details of registry values, profiles, configtool  parameters of region1 has come to region2. Basis team will need significant amount of time to change this and aligned as per Region2. Also when we need to fail back again to Region1, that time also we need to change those details as per Region1.

Hence, instead of using direct AFS or ANF physical name its strongly advised to use windows DFS-N which will support the naming convention of all these File shares(Check Fig 3).

                                                                   Fig 3


And During Disaster, here is what can be achieved as target architecture ( Fig4):


                                                 Fig 4

With this, we can achieve significant reduction of RTO and completely reduce entire steps of changing registry values, profiles or configtool parameters which will help Basis team. During failback to Region1 also, same can be followed without any manual intervention.

NOTE: We still need to perform few parameters changes ( e.g ASCS, DB load balancers in default profiles).

High Level Technical Steps:

Here is the high level steps I tried to update :

  • Install and Configure DFS-N for /sapmnt and /trans shared FS which should point as per above diagram( Please refer : https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/storage/dfs-namespaces/dfs-overview)

  • Make sure newly DFS are pointing correctly from all the Azure Application Subnet VMs.

  • Install SAP HA system with File share Cluster mentioning DFS-N names.

  • Check window registry , profile parameters. Now all should point to DFS-N names and should not point to any physical AFS/ANF names.


NOTE: In case , SAP is already installed and we need to change from physical name to DFS-N names , then we need to change registry , profiles and configtool parameters manually which will be one time activity.

entry.title: Simplification List for Plant Maintenance (Enterprise Asset Management)
entry.content:
Introduction:


While delivering SAP S/4HANA on-premise services at customer site or when I meet any consultant from the implementation partner company, the first question I get is the list of pre-requisites to be handled before SAP S/4HANA transition with respect to Enterprise Asset Management (formerly Plant Maintenance. Henceforth it will be termed as EAM  in this blog) application. This happened many times & still I am getting this question. So, I thought of writing a blog which helps customers, partners, consultants, users & other stake holders with this frequently asked questions.

Simplification List/Items (SI checks):


Simplification list provides detailed functional-level descriptions of the required changes to individual transactions and solution capabilities in SAP S/4HANA. It supports customers who are converting from SAP ECC/on-premise edition to SAP S/4HANA. The Simplification List provides information on the activities customers need to complete before, during, and after the conversion to SAP S/4HANA.

This list is prepared during “prepare phase” of the transition. The list is available in the system using the TA: /SDF/RC_START_CHECK.  These pre-checks will check the usage of the existing SAP Business Suite system during the planning phase prior to the actual conversion to SAP S/4HANA, identify the relevant Simplification Items.

Different actions/steps have to be taken for different applications for the smooth SAP S/4HANA transition. SAP provides SAP S/4HANA Transition Worklist item ( which I will refer here in this blog as S4TWL ) NOTES for each item.

 

Transition phases to SAP S/4HANA:


SAP S/4HANA Transition Phases


 


Prepare Phase


The Simplification List, therefore, serves three purposes:

  • Planning the actual system conversion from a traditional ERP solution to SAP S/4HANA.

  • Reducing efforts during the actual conversion by taking necessary steps/actions for each simplification items in advance.

  • Avoids re-work by implementing SAP S/4HANA-compliant strategic capabilities (when rolling out new capabilities).


Note:

  • All the items mentioned below may not be valid for all the customers. Run the TA: /SDF/RC_START_CHECK to know the items which are valid for you.

  • Following simplification is valid as of SAP S/4HANA 2022. New items may be added in future.

  • Impact & validity of each simplification items will vary across different customers.


 

Simplification List for the EAM Application:


Overall list & comments:







































































SL # Title SAP Note # Result
1 S4TWL - Object List Number Field Length Extension 2580670 Increase in the DDIC length.
SUM tool will take care of this automatically.
2 S4TWL - LIS in EAM 2267463 Future PM analytics will be based on HANA & CDS views.
User can still access the classic LIS.
3 S4TWL - Download Data in MS Access out of List 2270076 Use MS Excel instead of MS Access DB.
4 S4TWL - Scheduling of Maintenance Plan 2270078 TA: IP30 & its report RISTRA20 still exists in SAP S/4HANA system. User can evaluate new TA:  IP30H & its new report RISTRA20H for reference.
5 S4TWL - Batch Input for Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) 2270107 TA IBIP is not support further.
Use corresponding BAPI’s.
6 S4TWL - Changes In List Reports For Order and Notification 2270108 TA: IW37 & IW49 exists in SAP S/4HANA system.
User can evaluate & use the new TA: IW37N & IW49N.
7 S4TWL - WebDynpro Application for Maintenance Planner (<= EhP4) 2270110 Use the new Maintenance Planner and Maintenance Worker Roles instead of maintenance technician role.
8 S4TWL - Enterprise Search in EAM 2270123 The way the search is performed is changed. No action is required.
9 S4TWL - SAP Graphics in EAM Transactions 2482659 Tabular or other formats display exists & no graphical display.
10 S4TWL - Mobile Asset Management (MAM) 2270080 Use "asset manager" instead of MAM.

 

Details of each item:




  1. S4TWL - Object List Number Field Length Extension




Note# 2580670

SAP has decided to extend the object list numbers (data element OBJKNR, domain OBJKN) from the data format INT4 to the data format INT8. This change affects all the tables (e.g: OBJK) that contain a field with a reference to the data element OBJKNR.

 









OBJK table in ECC System


 


OBJK table in SAP S/4HANA System

Required actions:

  • Its information only.

  • SUM will take care of it automatically.


 

2. S4TWL - LIS in EAM


Note# 2267463

Its an information only item where future analytical reports, transactions are based on HANA & CDS which simplifies the process by reducing the tables & improves the performance.

Required actions:

  • Enhancements and modifications of the LIS will not work for the future analytic solution.

  • Custom report will stop working after update which are accessing the tables S061 to S065, S070, S114 to S116.


 

3. S4TWL - Download Data in MS Access out of List


Note# 2270076

SAP GUI supports downloading data to MS Access. This functionality is not the target architecture. Though, this functionality is available in SAP S/4HANA on-premises but not considered as future technology.

 


Notification to MS Access DB


 


DB Details for storing Notification data


Required actions:

  • Stop downloading data to MS access.

  • Use download to Excel or Open Office. Within Excel / Open Office you can use the embedded tools for populating to DB.





4. S4TWL - Scheduling of Maintenance Plan


Note# 2270078

Maintenance scheduling transaction IP30 (Reports RISTRA20) uses the outdated technology & it is still available in SAP S/4HANA system but not considered for future. This transaction is replaced by new transaction & report -- IP30H & RISTRA20H.









TA: IP30H in SAP S/4HANA System

 

Required actions:

Check for the scheduled background jobs (SM37) with the old report RISTRA20. Delete those jobs & replace them with the new report (RISTRA20H).

 

5. S4TWL - Batch Input for Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)


Note# 2270107

Transaction IBIP uses Batch Input (outdated) technology to create transactional data for EAM objects. This is still available in SAP S/4HANA system but not considered for future.


TA: IBIP in both ECC and SAP S/4HANA systems


Required actions:

Plant maintenance offers lot of BAPI’s (mentioned in the note 2270107) to create transaction data. Create custom programs using these API’s to create EAM object data in the system instead of TA: IBIP.

 

6. S4TWL - Changes In List Reports For Order and Notification


Note# 2270108

Transactions IW37 & IW49 are w.r.t order operations are improved further (performance) in new TA: IW37n & IW49n. Though the existing TA’s IW37 & IW49 are available in the SAP S/4HANA system but are not considered for future.


TA: IW37N and IW49N


Required Actions:

  • run the reports 'QM_PHASE_FILL' and 'RIAFVC_IPHAS_FILL' before the upgrade to SAP S/4HANA.

  • start using the new TA: IW37n & IW49n instead of IW37 & IW49.


 

7. S4TWL - WebDynpro Application for Maintenance Planner (<= EhP4)


Note# 2270110

Maintenance Technician role is obsolete for SAP S/4HANA release. Though it is still available in SAP S/4HANA but not in future.

Use the new Maintenance Planner and Maintenance Worker Roles available in SAP S/4HANA.

Required Actions:

Activate the Business Functions (LOG_EAM_SIMPLICITY_2 to 6) in TA: SFW5 to get the full functionality of Maintenance Planner and Maintenance Worker Role.


SFW5 in ECC System


 


SFW5 in SAP S/4HANA System


 

8. S4TWL - Enterprise Search in EAM


Note# 2270123

With SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition, the Enterprise Search models are now replication free search models using HDB. This means reliance on TREX & other replication data processes are eliminated. Now, the searches are based on SAP S/4HANA DB tables directly.

Required Actions:

Check the note 2270123 to follow the mentioned steps to activate the search models or if you are intending to use value help within WebDynpro App.


Enterprise Search in S/4 HANA



9. S4TWL - SAP Graphics in EAM Transactions


Note# 2482659

SAP Business Graphics used in Plant Maintenance applications are no longer supported in SAP S/4HANA. Example of affected TA: IP19.


TA: IP19 in ECC


 


TA: IP19 in SAP S/4HANA


Other affected transactions are available as the PDF attachment in the NOTE.

Required Actions:

Use the tabular display.

 

10. S4TWL - Mobile Asset Management (MAM)


Note# 2270080

The 'Mobile Asset Management' solution is not supported in S/4HANA. User can use the fiori apps on mobile or use “asset manager” (additional license required).

 

Additional Information:


Simplification items check:

  • can be run manually in advance on the actual SAP ERP system.

  • It is necessary to resolve all "red" inconsistency checks, otherwise the system conversion will fail.

  • will be run by the Software Update Manager (SUM) at least twice during the conversion procedure


Next Steps:


This blog detailed out the list of EAM items which are changed or deleted in SAP S/4HANA product compared to Ehp/ECC product & consultants are required to demo these changes to stake holders before SAP S/4HANA transition so that their daily work is not affected by these changes.

Overall, I have shared the contents with respect to all the EAM simplification items which are valid till SAP S/4HANA 2022.

entry.title: Plan Your Conversion to SAP S/4HANA – Expert Guided Implementation
entry.content:
Is your organization planning to take the next big digital move from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition? Are you wondering where to start and curious about what’s involved in this journey? You are in the right place!

Transforming your digital business core from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA could be complex but rewarding - so is the process of planning such a project. Planning an SAP S/4HANA conversion project involves various tools, teams, and expertise for a smooth and successful Go-Live. This is where SAP Enterprise Support Academy helps you in being informed of all the tools, technologies, and techniques you need to consume and apply along different phases of the project.

The EGI “Plan Your Conversion to SAP S/4HANA” covers a range of topics that are key to planning your SAP S/4HANA conversion project. The session answers topics like how SAP S/4HANA is better and different from SAP ERP, discusses the different editions of SAP S/4HANA and how to plan and successfully execute the transition to SAP S/4HANA. This session also highlights the starting points and best practices for the conversion.

What is an Expert Guided Implementation? 


An expert-guided implementation (EGI) is a remote training session delivered virtually over several days. For further details on the EGI format and a list of the SAP S/4HANA related expert-guided implementation training sessions, see the blog: Expert-Guided Implementation (EGI) – The practical training option

When should I attend this Expert Guided Implementation? 


Customers usually attend this EGI during the Discover phase of their SAP S/4HANA conversion project. From the session, you will know how the data model of SAP S/4HANA is simplified and better suited for running your business processes simpler and faster. We will also discuss about SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and On-Premise deployment options and what “RISE with SAP” has to offer. You will be equipped with all the essential planning elements of your conversion project by the end of this EGI.

* Before you can access the registration links, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found  here

What topics will be covered in this EGI?


A summary of the contents is listed below. For the full schedule see the abstract listed here

Plan Your Conversion to SAP S/4HANA*


Day 1 Major Topics



  • SAP S/4HANA introduction

  • Transition scenarios to SAP S/4HANA

  • Key activities and planning elements of an SAP S/4HANA conversion project


Day 2 Major Topics



  • SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA

  • Simplification Items Check

  • Functional Preparations – Finance, Customers and Vendors


Day 3 Major Topics



  • SAP Maintenance Planner

  • Custom code activities in SAP S/4HANA Conversion Projects

  • Recommended activities for SAP Fiori and SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics


Day 4 Major Topics



  • Software Update Manager (SUM) and DMO Option of SUM

  • Downtime Minimization Approaches

  • SAP HANA 2

  • Target System Sizing


What are the key benefits of attending this EGI? 


This EGI is power packed with a lot of essential information and knowledge pointers that you need right from the start of the project until the Go-Live on SAP S/4HANA. Attending this EGI will also reduce the time required to upskill for planning an SAP S/4HANA Conversion project.  At the end of the EGI, you will be empowered with the approaches and techniques you need to apply to efficiently convert your SAP ERP systems to SAP S/4HANA. To attend one of the upcoming sessions, Register Now*

Further information on Expert-Guided Implementations to support your SAP S/4HANA journey



For a list of SAP S/4HANA related Expert-Guided Implementation sessions see blog: Expert-Guided Implementation (EGI) – The practical training option.

View the full Expert Guided Implementation portfolio here > Complete Portfolio

You’ll find much more in the relevant value maps, see blog: SAP S/4HANA: Let’s Get Moving with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.

Before you can access the value maps or the EGI registration links, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

If you have any further questions or comments on the contents of this EGI, please feel free to post them in the section below and I hope to see you soon in one of our Plan Your Conversion to SAP S/4HANA - EGIs!

entry.title: Near-Zero Downtime Maintenance for High Availability / Disaster Recovery (HA/DR) SAP Java Systems
entry.content:
Overview

Being up-to-date with the latest technologies and aiming the production stability and consistency are the top priorities for most businesses. However, that often requires business critical downtime for production systems. SAP offers a solution to this problem by providing its clients with the near-Zero Downtime Maintenance (nZDM) for SAP Java procedure for its SAP Java systems.

This blog gives an overview of the nZDM Java solution and the specifics of performing it on High Availability / Disaster Recovery (HA/DR) SAP Java systems.

 

What is the nZDM Java procedure?

The near-Zero Downtime Maintenance for SAP Java is a procedure that enables you to perform maintenance activities with greatly reduced business downtime on SAP Portal, SAP Process Orchestration (PO) and SAP systems that have only some of the usage types part of SAP Process Orchestration. nZDM Java deliveries are part of SAP SL Toolset program.

The nZDM Java procedure allows SAP customers to perform all maintenance activities on a copy or clone of the system (also called ‘target system’ in the context of nZDM) while the production system (called ‘source system’) is still in use.

More information about the nZDM solution can be found here:

nZDM Java: minimize the business downtime of your SAP Process Orchestration maintenance activity

 

Approaches for performing nZDM Java

Depending on the client’s system landscape and complexity, there are two main approaches for performing nZDM Java. Both rely on the copy or clone of the production system and include some basic common steps but the finalization steps differ.

Below the different approaches are explained via diagrams. The following legend describes the meaning of the different colors:


 

  • System switch


This approach is suitable for virtualized systems as well as HA/DR systems. The system’s clone will become the new production system. Before replacing the production system with the target system, testing the updated clone is possible without downtime. This happens in a special ‘nZDM test mode’.

The technical downtime is approximately the time needed to restart the system. It varies depending on the type and load of the system.

These are the generic steps of performing a ‘System Switch’ on a standard Process Orchestration system:

 

0. Initial State

The system on which we perform the ‘System Switch’ is a SAP Process Orchestration (PO) system consisting of several components: one or more Java Application Servers (AS), SAP Web Dispatcher (WD), SAP Central Services (SCS), SAP Enqueue Replication Service (ERS) and a database.


 

1. Preparation

Prepare the production system for nZDM Java: configure database settings, deactivate background jobs, set the landscape directory (SLD) to read-only mode, download and run the nZDM Java GUI on a separate host.

 

2. Connect the nZDM Java GUI to the source (production) system

Connect the nZDM Java GUI to one of the application servers of the source system.



 

3. Start recording

Initialize recording of the database changes on the production system.


 

4. Clone the source (production) system

Clone the source (production) system to create a target system.



 

5. Isolate the cloned system

Configure the isolation (network fencing) of the target system to avoid conflict with the source (production) system.

 

6. Start the target system

When the isolated clone is started, it starts as a target system.



 

7. Update the target system

Perform the desired maintenance activities on the target system.



 

8. (optional) Test the updated system

The updated target system can be tested before replacing the production system. No production system downtime is required for while testing on the target system. However, before performing tests it is important to do a backup of the target system. After the tests, the target system is restored to that backup.


 

9. Connect nZDM Java GUI to the target system



 

10. Start DB data replication from the source system to the target system

Replicate all available data changes from the source’s DB to the target system.



 

11. Stop source system / enter downtime phase

To replicate the last data changes the source system is stopped, starting the downtime phase. Only the DB of the source system remains active so that the replication can be finished.

This phase is activated via the nZDM GUI.



 

12. Stop and unfence target system

After finishing the replication, the updated target system is stopped and unfenced. Once unfenced, it can be started.

 

13. Start updated system / end of downtime phase

After starting the updated system, it replaces the original and becomes the new production system.



 

  • Database switch


Recommended when there is no virtualization of the system and not suitable for High Availability / Disaster Recovery systems. It uses the newly created database on the existing source system DB host. The data is cloned on the existing database server or on a shared storage. This approach is useful when the existing productive environment has a large scale and complex configurations. Therefore it might be the least costly way to use the already existing environment.

 

nZDM Java on High Availability / Disaster Recovery (HA/DR) SAP Java System

Depending on the scenario and the suitable approach for it, the steps of the nZDM Java procedure may differ. This section contains a step-by-step description of a successfully performed scenario of nZDM Java on HA/DR systems.

 

nZDM Java on HA/DR System: Cluster Clone

This approach for nZDM Java on HA/DR system is more suitable for virtualized systems or other environments where making a clone or a copy of the system can be easily performed. With the HA/DR ‘Cluster Clone’ the update is performed on a clone / copy of the production system. During the maintenance performed on of the clone (target system), the production system (source system) is active. The downtime is required for completion of the replication of data changes from source to target and takes about one system restart time.

These are the generic steps of performing a ‘Cluster Clone’ on a two-node HA/DR system setup:

 

0. Initial State

The system on which we perform the ‘Cluster Clone’ is a HA/DR SAP Java system with two nodes and multiple application servers. Each node may have SAP Web Dispatcher (WD), SAP Central Services (SCS), SAP Enqueue Replication Service (ERS) and a database. The nodes are bound together in a HA/DR cluster via HA/DR software.

There is only one active WD, SCS, ERS and DB instance in the cluster of nodes at any given moment. If an active instance fails, the inactive activates and replaces it.



 

1. Preparation

Prepare the production system for nZDM Java: configure database settings, deactivate background jobs, set the landscape directory (SLD) to read-only mode, download and run the nZDM Java GUI on a separate host.

 

2. Connect nZDM Java GUI to the source (production) system

Connect the nZDM Java GUI to one of the application servers of the source system.



 

3. Start recording

Initialize recording of the database changes on the production system.

 

4. Clone the source (production) system

Clone the whole cluster of nodes to create a target system.



 

5. Isolate the cloned system

Configure the isolation (network fencing) of the target system to avoid conflict with the source (production) system.

 

6. Start the target system

When the isolated clone is started, it becomes our target system.



 

7. Update the target system

Perform the desired maintenance activities on the target system.



 

8. (optional) Test the updated system

The updated target system can be tested before replacing the production system. No production system downtime is required for while testing on the target system. However, before performing tests it is important to do a backup of the target system. After the tests, the target system is restored to that backup.

 

9. Connect nZDM Java GUI to the target system



 

10. Start DB data replication from the source system to the target system

Begin replication of data changes from the source’s DB to the target system.



 

11. Stop source system / enter downtime phase

To replicate the last data changes the source system is stopped, starting the downtime phase. Only the DB of the source system remains active so that the replication can be finished.

This phase is activated via the nZDM GUI.



 

12. Stop and unfence target system

After finishing the replication, the updated target system is stopped and unfenced. Once unfenced, it can be started.

 

13. Start updated system / end of downtime phase

After starting the updated system, it replaces the original and becomes the new production system.



 

Conclusion

The nZDM Java procedure offers a flexible and effective solution for updating SAP Java systems. It also supports various High Availability / Disaster Recovery landscapes of SAP Java systems. The main benefits of the procedure are the significantly reduced downtime and the ability to test the updated system before making it a production system, minimizing the risk of possible problems.

 

Further Information

nZDM Java user guides and the nZDM Java GUI are available at http://service.sap.com/sltoolset

Minimizing planned downtime during maintenance

What is High Availability system?
